narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD weakness because DXY CSM sits at zero on H4 with USD proxy at -0.58 and H4 sell signals across USDJPY, USDCHF, and USDCAD confirming broad dollar supply
2. JPY underperformance because JPY ranks weakest among non-USD currencies at D1 CSM 61 and fading, with all JPY crosses generating H4 sell signals driven by USDJPY correlation cluster above 0.91
3. Risk-on equity and commodity bid because S&P 500 at 7230 rising, silver up 3.29%, gold at 4630 rising, and copper firm collectively suppress safe-haven demand and support growth-linked currencies

PRICE CONFIRMS

GBPUSD is the lead long with D1 and H4 both buy-aligned and GBP ranking second strongest on D1 CSM at 91, though H4 CSM for GBP is fading to 40, suggesting the pair is extended in the near term. NZD and AUD are confirming the anti-dollar narrative through their 0.93 correlation and matching H4 buy signals, with NZD leading on H4 CSM at 100. USDCHF H4 and D1 both sell-aligned with CHF the strongest D1 currency at 100, but H4 CHF CSM has collapsed to 10, revealing CHF strength is stalling and the pair may be losing momentum on the downside.

REGIME

Risk-On confirmed, with S&P 500 up 0.29% at record-like levels, VIX contained at 17 despite a minor uptick, and gold rising alongside equities in a commodity-led reflation rather than fear-driven bid.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. CHF strength vs CHF momentum: D1 CSM ranks CHF at 100 but H4 CSM has dropped to 10, meaning the dominant strong-CHF narrative is not being sustained intraday and USDCHF downside may be near exhausted
2. DXY rising 0.13% vs universal dollar sell signals: price shows a marginal DXY uptick while every USD pair H4 signal points to dollar selling, creating a short-term directional conflict that requires resolution before high-conviction USD shorts are added

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: GBPUSD up - D1 and H4 both buy, GBP strongest D1 currency with risk-on backdrop - Conviction 7/10
Secondary: NZDUSD up - NZD leads H4 CSM at 100, risk-on confirmed, AUDUSD correlation reinforces - Conviction 6/10
Avoid: USDCHF - H4 CHF momentum collapsing despite D1 CHF dominance creates directional trap
Watch: DXY breaking below 98.00 confirms dollar sell signals and validates both long positions immediately
key_observation: Conflicting USD signals: dollar rebounds on trade tensions while soft commodities surge on dollar weakness. Record equity highs support risk-on sentiment, but energy weakness and mixed livestock suggest selective commodity strength rather than broad reflation.
watch: Trade tension escalation, US-Iran negotiation outcome, equity momentum sustainability, commodity demand trends particularly in soft commodities
